title: pogogyne douglasii subsp. parviflora
intro: Pogogyne douglasii subsp. parviflora, also known as Douglas' synogamy, is a plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the western United States, where it grows as a perennial shrub. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Pogogyne douglasii subsp. parviflora include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for use.
